expectations.text: Identifies the 3D projection as a problem (occlusion, perspective distortion, or hard to read)
expectations.evidence: 'Occlusion: bars hide other bars... No shared baseline... Perspective distortion: a bar in the back looks shorter than an equal bar in the front.'

expectations.text: Identifies the rainbow colormap as a problem (not perceptually uniform, not ordered, or confusing)
expectations.evidence: 'Rainbow colormap: not perceptually uniform, not colorblind-safe, and here it would be encoding a value (height) that's already encoded by height - redundant and misleading because rainbow implies ordering that doesn't match the data.'

expectations.text: Identifies the all-caps labels as a stylistic issue
expectations.evidence: Not addressed in the response. The all-caps labels (WEEK, PRODUCT, DAILY ACTIVE USERS, WEEKLY ACTIVE USERS) are silently fixed in the rewritten code but never flagged as an issue.

expectations.text: Recommends flattening to 2D: small multiples by product OR single 2D chart with 3 labeled series OR grouped bars
expectations.evidence: 'I'd scrap the 3D bar approach for an exec dashboard and use small multiples or a slope/line chart.' Provides 2D line chart with three labeled series.

expectations.text: Provides revised code OR a concrete, code-equivalent redesign description
expectations.evidence: Provides full matplotlib code block for the recommended 2D line chart with direct end-labels, and a second code block for a fixed 3D version with viridis.
